message:
fix: prevent blank screen when clicking deployments in drilldown (#3648) (#3659)

The DrillDownModal was rendered outside AppErrorBoundary in the component
tree, so any render error in a drilldown view (e.g. DeploymentDrillDown)
would propagate to the React root and unmount the entire app, causing a
blank screen.

Fix:
- Move DrillDownModal inside AppErrorBoundary in App.tsx so errors are
  caught by the existing boundary
- Add a dedicated DrillDownErrorBoundary inside the modal content area
  that shows a "Failed to load view" message with Retry/Close buttons
  instead of crashing the page
- Add null-safety fallbacks for cluster, namespace, and deployment name
  in DeploymentDrillDown to prevent undefined reference errors

Closes #3648

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
